---
id: Q-03
topic: Decisions dir ownership — who writes Q-*.md?
stage: narrative
blocking: true
---
## Context

Decision-mode slides need a `decisions/*.md` source. The question: who writes
these? The maker agents when they hit ambiguity, the orchestrator when it
gates a phase transition, or the user by hand? Evidence in
`docs/plan/2026-04-17-preso-editable-preview.md:260-267` and the interactive
protocol at `.claude/skills/cast-interactive-questions/SKILL.md:42-45`
("read artifacts first, form an opinion, THEN ask") which argues for the
agent-as-author pattern.

## Recommended

- **Option A — Maker + orchestrator write, the user answers:** The agents that
  discover the ambiguity are best-placed to frame it. Matches the
  interactive-questions protocol at
  `.claude/skills/cast-interactive-questions/SKILL.md:42`. the user-by-hand is
  fallback for cross-agent questions that outlive any one run.

## Alternatives

- **Option B — the user writes, agents only read:** Pushes the framing burden onto
  the user. Defeats the "agents frame their own gaps" pattern documented in
  `docs/plan/2026-04-17-preso-editable-preview.md:264`.
- **Option C — New sub-agent just for question authoring:** Over-engineered
  until we have evidence the maker agents can't frame their own gaps. See
  `agents/agent-design-guide/SKILL.md` §11 ("no hypothetical future work").
- **Option D — Hybrid: agents draft, the user refines inline before review:** Adds
  a step the user would inevitably skip. Keeps the worst of both worlds. Reference:
  `docs/plan/2026-04-17-preso-editable-preview.md:262`.
